title: Figure 1 from: Ballardini M, Mercuri A, Littardi C, Abbas S, Couderc M, Ludeña B, Pintaud J (2013) The chloroplast DNA locus psbZ-trnfM as a potential barcode marker in Phoenix L. (Arecaceae). ZooKeys 365: 71-82. https://doi.org/10.3897/zookeys.365.5725
author: Ballardini, Marco
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Figure 1 - The sequenced cpDNA psbZ-trnfM region.The location of PCR primers used and polymorphisms found in this study are shown. DNA fragment length refers to the Phoenix dactylifera cv. Khalas cpDNA sequence (Yang et al. 2010), characterised by a 4-repetitions minisatellite haplotype (NCBI Reference Sequence: NC_013991.2).
steps_raw: Figure 1 - The sequenced cpDNA psbZ-trnfM region.The location of PCR primers used and polymorphisms found in this study are shown. DNA fragment length refers to the Phoenix dactylifera cv. Khalas cpDNA sequence (Yang et al. 2010), characterised by a 4-repetitions minisatellite haplotype (NCBI Reference Sequence: NC_013991.2).
